title: <div>  •  Adrian (Rocky) – Rocky’s emotional anchor, giving him a sense of purpo
content:
• Adrian (Rocky) – Rocky’s emotional anchor, giving him a sense of purpose beyond the ring.

Key Traits:
• Represents balance, love, or human connection.
• Often sacrificed for the protagonist’s greater goal.
• Can serve as a tragic consequence of obsession.

5. The System (The Uncaring Machine)

This is the school, competition, industry, or larger force that the protagonist must navigate. It doesn’t care about individuals—it only values results.

Examples:
• Shaffer Conservatory (Whiplash) – A cutthroat world where only perfection matters.
• The Boxing Industry (Raging Bull) – A corrupt and punishing system that devours its fighters.
• The Feudal Samurai System (Vagabond) – A world where only the strong survive.

Key Traits:
• Cold, indifferent, rewards cruelty.
• Forces the protagonist to adapt or be crushed.
• May represent larger societal expectations.

Genre Conventions, Story Types, and Shapes
context: From document: Analyzing the Common Threads in Whiplash, Real, and Vagabond — chunk 13 (chunk 13/14)
